category_title: Students who engage in active instructional avoidance through disruptive and off-task behaviors
minimal_context: Martin is participating in an independent reading block where the expectation is to read assigned materials quietly.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Martin, it is time for independent reading. Please open your assigned reading materials and work quietly.

STUDENT: (Turns his body completely away from the assigned reading materials and looks around the room)

TUTOR: Martin, please focus on your reading.

STUDENT: (Stands up from his desk and walks over to the student sitting next to him) Hey, what are you looking at?

TUTOR: Martin, the expectation right now is to be quiet.

STUDENT: (Remains out of his seat, keeping his eyes and body directed away from his own desk, and continues talking to his peer) I don't want to read, let's talk about recess.

options.A: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the independent reading block and the student's overall intervention content.
options.B: Emphasize student self-selection of all texts and reading materials to be used during the independent reading block.
options.C: Purposefully select assigned reading materials written by and/or featuring people of color for the independent reading block.
options.D: Meet with a coordinator at the beginning and end of each day to review goals and trade points for incentives.
